spatialLayoutNotes.guardrail: To support an intuitive left-to-right scanning motion across the room's workspace artwork, both physical interactable targets are anchored to the left side of the viewport.

hotspots.plantPot: Positioned directly to the left of the main desk bounding box.
hotspots.lockedDrawer: Positioned on the far-left screen border.

uiState.centerViewport: Dark office scene. All room assets unclickable.

phases.notebookText:
Thanks so much for helping me patch my terminal systems while I'm away, Rookie! ૮꒰˶ᵔ ᵕ ᵔ˶꒱ა 

I actually left a little welcome gift for you in my office! The bad news? I think I forgot to take it out of my super-secret locked drawer before I stepped out ૮꒰˶´ ꒳ `꒱ა. But don't worry, the key is hidden somewhere in the room! 

I usually start all my big investigations by telling my terminal to run an environment sweep. Try typing this exact single-parameter command into the console below to map our workspace:
**`self.search("look around room")`**
phases.terminalOutput:
[SYSTEM]: Scanning local grid coordinates...
[SUCCESS]: Environment data parsed successfully. 
[LOG]: 2 physical assets mapped to volatile memory.

characterDialogue.speaker: Bill the Lizard
characterDialogue.text: Terrific. Ten minutes on the clock and Bunny's already got me running automated sweeps. Looks like the terminal indexed two local objects into memory: plantPot and lockedDrawer. Both on my left. Let's inspect the plant first.

uiState.centerViewport: Bounding boxes pulse green around Plant (left of desk) and Drawer (far left).

hotspotData.hoverTooltip: [Interact]: Is there something hidden under this plantPot?

phases.notebookText:
Sometimes a basic sweep isn't enough—we need to be much more specific! We can pass multiple arguments into our search behaviors by separating them with a **comma**. 

For example: `self.search("behind", someObject)`. 

But you have to be super careful! The data entries inside our system are incredibly sensitive... you could say they are **caseSensitive** ૮꒰ - ﻌ •꒱ა! JavaScript cares immensely about lowercase and uppercase letters. Also, parameter order matters completely! Your target direction string *always* comes first, and the environmental object variable comes second.

Let's look directly beneath the plant: **`self.search("under", plantPot)`**
phases.terminalOutput:
[SYSTEM]: Evaluating relative spatial parameters...
[LOG]: Position offset applied to object target: plantPot.
[SUCCESS]: Target physical asset uncovered.

characterDialogue.speaker: Bill the Lizard
characterDialogue.text: Hiding a physical brass key right under the decor. Truly cutting-edge security, Inspector. Alright, let's take this key object and try it on the lockedDrawer variable on the far left.

phases.terminalOutput:
[TERMINAL ERROR]: TypeError: Assignment to constant variable.
[CRITICAL]: Execution halted. Cannot alter immutable object state.

characterDialogue.speaker: Bill the Lizard
characterDialogue.text: You've got to be kidding me. The physical key slots straight into the lock cylinder, but the internal terminal software just threw a mutability error and froze the mechanism. Look at the tracker—why is a desk drawer hardcoded against modifications as a const? Let me look at Page 3 of Bunny's notes.

uiState.centerViewport: Red padlock overlay active on drawer layer.

phases.notebookText:
Oh no! I completely forgot to mention our precinct security layout! ૮꒰˶´ ꒳ `꒱ა 

To protect core office objects from being altered by outside hackers, our database initializes environmental references using `const`. A variable declared with **`const`** is immutable—meaning once its initial value is assigned, it can absolutely never be changed! Look at your tracker panel; it's locked down tight as a constant.

If we want an object's properties or status to change dynamically during an investigation, we must declare it using **`let`**, which lets our terminal modify its data state!

Let's override the restriction on the drawer by re-scoping it in our current terminal layer. Type this line:
**`let lockedDrawer`**
phases.terminalOutput:
[SUCCESS]: Variable scope override successful.
[LOG]: Identifier 'lockedDrawer' reallocated to mutable memory space.

characterDialogue.speaker: Bill the Lizard
characterDialogue.text: Alright, the registry tracking grid updated. The drawer is no longer marked as an immutable constant. Let's run that unlock command one more time to break through to Phase 5.

uiState.centerViewport: Red padlock overlay removed from drawer asset layer.

phases.terminalOutput:
[SYSTEM]: Executing function unlock() on target variable...
[SUCCESS]: Lock cylinder rotated 180°. Structural pins disengaged.
[LOG]: Interactive sequence completed. Drawer opened.

uiState.centerViewport: Baker Sheep character asset dynamically enters workspace from right, pathfinding to desk center overlay.

phases.notebookText: Execute a warning message to print your true workplace role directly to the terminal interface.
phases.expectedPlayerInput: console.warn("I am just a lizard technician")
phases.terminalOutput: [WARNING]: I am just a lizard technician

narrativeSequence.title: The Client Bursts In
narrativeSequence.speaker: Baker Sheep
narrativeSequence.text: Help! Help! Someone broke into my pastry shop down the main street! The flour is everywhere, my terminal network lines are cut, my receipts are totally corrupted! Detective, you have to grab your badge and help me find the thief!

narrativeSequence.title: Clarifying Professional Identity
narrativeSequence.uiOverlay: System Hint Prompt over Bill's portrait frame
narrativeSequence.hintText: I need to make sure this panicked pastry chef knows I am simply an underpaid network technician, not the chief detective...

conversation.speaker: Baker Sheep
conversation.text: I don't care if you're a technician, a database engineer, or an alligator! The Chief Detective's chair is empty, but your terminal console is fully logged in! You're the only person in this building with a working console window! Please, I'll pay you in fresh cinnamon rolls, hot biscuits, glazed turnovers—whatever you want! Just come audit my kitchen terminals before the logs wipe themselves!

conversation.speaker: Bill the Lizard
conversation.text: (Sighs heavily, pulling off his IT maintenance visor) Look, I'm just supposed to be running software updates today... but if your store transaction logs are actively corrupted, I can at least dump your raw database files to a backup drive before they overwrite. Keep your biscuits warm, pal. Let's go look at your bakery.
